LAMBERT SCORES ENGINEER


Lambert said that he had con.
tended for two years that Ralston
was untitled for the place. Are
we going to have repetition of the
Sprague avenue fill on the new
Monroe street bridge?" asked Lam.
bert. Already there has been ex.
pended on the bridge some $S1 900
and what is there to show for it?
We can not tell what the Sprague
avenue fill will cost before it is
finally completed. Ralston has
cost the taxpayers in two years
100000, and if he continues in
office he may bankrupt the city be-
fore he gets through"


c. w. Mohr: "A year ago the
matter of the removal of Ralston
was up and had I-voted for it then
we might have terminated this
trouble at that time. ] am sorry
now that | did not favor the move
| want Mr. Ostrander to point to
a single job of any size built by
Ralston that was finished in accord.
ance with the plans and within the
estimate. There is not one. The
city council has time and again
been fooled-bunkoed by the
smooth talk of Ralston."


Councilman Shaefer said that
since the court had placed the re-
sponsibility for the Sprague avenue
muddle on the shoulders of Ralston
he accepted the judgment of the
court He had examined the papers
in the case and conceded with the
findings of the court. But he OPP
posed the removal of RalstOn at
this time because of the amount of
work pending,


Ostrander presented a lot of fig
ures on work that the engineers
office turned out last year and had
under way at the present as an
argument for the retention of Raj
ston, all of which Lambert insisted
was further proof that competent
man was needed to handle these
large undertakings.


When the final vote of the coun-
Gil removing Ralston was an
nounced there was a loud cheer of
approval from the large audience
present.
